mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high PAYEMS regimes show no significant outperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger corporate capacity utilization and economic activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO's 60-day forward returns show negative or insignificant correlation (Rank IC < 0.05) with SPX performance.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 2.5% in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show positive correlation with combined CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS spikes (Rank IC > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate stronger demand for residential construction, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns fail to outperform baseline when HOUST exceeds 550,000 units (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) signals a resilient economy, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show negative or insignificant correlation with RSAFS/UNRATE combinations (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining GDP growth (GDPC1) signals weakening economic demand, which correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show positive correlation with GDPC1 declines (Rank IC > 0.1).
